mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% in low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce a positive return frequency above 58% over the 60-day window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth coincides with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 45% of observations during expansionary phases.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates stable employment and wage income, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth periods yield a positive return frequency below 55% or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.3 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Inflation spikes above the threshold are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in > 65% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP expansion and rising retail sales validate sustained aggregate demand, creating a positive earnings trajectory that drives S&P 500 forward returns higher over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising GDP and retail sales fails to generate a positive return frequency above 60% over the 20-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during late-cycle expansions signals capacity utilization peaks, which historically precedes positive short-term equity momentum over 5 trading days before supply constraints emerge.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production momentum during late-cycle phases yields negative 5-day SPX returns in > 55% of observations.
